message:
test(readme): JoinType + MergeValidateMode variant coverage

Self-review of merge surface caught only JoinType::Inner and
MergeValidateMode::OneToOne being exercised by integration tests.
The 4 other JoinType variants and 3 other MergeValidateMode variants
had ZERO coverage despite being core pandas merge surface.

Adds readme_join_type_round_trip — reachable via prelude alone — that
drives all 5 JoinType variants against frames with partial key overlap
(df1 keys 1,2,3; df2 keys 2,3,4):

- Inner → 2 rows (intersect)
- Left  → 3 rows (df1 keys preserved)
- Right → 3 rows (df2 keys preserved)
- Outer → 4 rows (union)
- Cross → 9 rows (3×3 cartesian, empty key list)

And the 3 untested MergeValidateMode variants:

- ManyToOne (df1 may dup, df2 unique) → 3 rows succeed
- ManyToMany (both may dup) → 3 rows succeed
- OneToMany (df1 unique, df2 may dup) → 3 rows succeed

Each variant's row count is asserted against the algebraic expected
value, so a regression in any join algorithm or validate-mode check
surfaces immediately.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.29.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
